title: AI Operating System for Web Design Agencies
metaDescription: Install and run an AI Operating System for your web design agency. We handle lead reply, quotes, project updates, site care reporting, and invoice chase so designers can ship.
excerpt: An AI Operating System is the coordination layer that runs the work around shipping the actual design. Here is what we install, in what order, and what done looks like.

sections.intro:
A web design agency runs on three loops: pitch new work, deliver projects on a deadline, and keep existing clients on retainer happy. Each loop has the same overhead: respond to leads, scope and quote, push status updates, chase invoices, write monthly recaps. Most of it is not design work. It is the coordination around it.

An AI Operating System (AIOS) is the layer that runs that coordination so designers and developers can ship. For a web design agency, that means every lead is answered in under 60 seconds with your booking link, every quote gets followed up, every project kicks off the same way, and monthly site care reports draft themselves from analytics and uptime data.

This page is the overview. Below is what we install, in what order, and what done looks like.
sections.fourCFit:
An AIOS is built on four layers, installed in this order:

  • Context: the system knows the agency. Your service tiers, your typical retainer scope, your voice with clients, the standards you hold.
  • Connections: the system reaches your tools. Your inbox and calendar, your CRM, your project tool (ClickUp, Asana, Notion), your invoicing (Stripe, FreshBooks, QuickBooks), your analytics and uptime monitor.
  • Capabilities: done-for-you workflows. Reply to a new lead. Send a discovery and quote package. Push milestone updates. Draft a monthly site care report. Chase an unpaid invoice.
  • Cadence: it runs on a schedule. A morning brief on partner desks. A weekly project status to each client. A monthly retainer report on the first.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
Done for a web design agency typically looks like this in the first 90 days:

  • Every inbound lead gets a personalized 60-second reply with your booking link. See 60-Second Lead Response for Web Design Agencies.
  • Monthly site care or retainer reports draft from live analytics and uptime data. See Automated Client Reporting for Web Design Agencies.
  • Inbound questions get triaged and drafted as replies so the team responds faster with less context-switching. See Inbox Triage and Reply Drafts for Agencies.
  • CRM stays in sync without anyone copying data between tools. See CRM Sync Without Copy-Paste for Agencies.
sections.howWeInstall:
Three phases:

  1. Audit, free, about 10 minutes. Score your AI readiness across the Four Cs and surface the three highest-leverage automations for your studio. Run the audit.
  2. Install, 10 business days. We wire up your tools, capture your studio voice and standards, and ship your first working automations. The free 60-Second Lead Engine is usually first.
  3. Operate, ongoing. We run it, watch it, fix it, and ship one new automation per week, measured against your kickoff baseline. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
For a typical web design agency in the 3 to 25 staff range, we plan against this baseline:

  • Recover 40 or more hours a month inside the team's calendar, or we keep working free until you do.
  • Lead response time from hours to under 60 seconds.
  • Site care reporting time per client down by 70 to 90 percent.
  • Project status visibility from on-demand to always-on.

We do not promise revenue lift. We promise recovered hours and the SLA.
sections.faq:
Who owns the system?

You do. Every prompt, connection, and automation lives in your accounts. If you ever stop working with us, you keep all of it.

What if something breaks?

We monitor every workflow and fix it. You get alerts and a clear log of every run, no silent failures.

How fast until ROI?

First automations are live within 10 business days. We measure recovered hours against your kickoff baseline over the following 30 days. The Lead Engine alone usually pays back within the first booked call.
